methods.description: Analysis of publicly available transcriptomic data for CabZIP genes to gain insight into their spatial and temporal expression profiles during development and stress responses.

steps.description: The transcriptome data of 59 CabZIPs genes (not including CabZIP25) from the Zunla-1 genome, were obtained using the Pepper Hub (http://pepperhub.hzau.edu.cn/) (Kim et al., 2014).
steps.searchable_summary: obtain transcriptomic data for CabZIP genes from Pepper Hub database
outputs: Transcriptomic data files for 59 CabZIP genes

steps.description: Identify data sets corresponding to different pepper tissues/organs at various developmental stages within the obtained transcriptome data, including leaves (L1-9), flowers (F1-9), fruits (FST0), pericarps (G1-11), placenta (T3-11), and seeds (S3-11).
steps.searchable_summary: identify and subset transcriptomic data for developmental stages and tissues
outputs: Subset of transcriptomic data for developmental stages/tissues

steps.description: Identify data sets corresponding to leaves/roots under different stress or hormone treatments within the obtained transcriptome data, such as cold (FL1-6/FR1-6), heat (HL1-6/HR1-6), salt (NaCl; NL1-6/NR1-6), osmotic (mannitol; ML1-6/MR1-6), ABA (AL1-6/AR1-6), gibberellic acid (GA3; G1-6L/GR1-6), indole acetic acid (IAA; IL1-6/IR1-6), JA (JL1-6/JR1-6), and SA (SL1-6/SR1-6).
steps.searchable_summary: identify and subset transcriptomic data for stress and hormone treatments
outputs: Subset of transcriptomic data for stress/hormone treatments

steps.description: Two separate projects were created, one for the developmental tissues that included data from six organs (leaf, flower, fruit, pericarp, placenta, and seed) and the other for four stress treatments including NaCl, mannitol, heat, and cold stress, and five phytohormone treatments including IAA, GA3, ABA, SA, and JA.
steps.searchable_summary: organize transcriptomic data into separate projects for developmental and stress/hormone conditions
inputs: Subset of transcriptomic data for developmental stages/tissues
Subset of transcriptomic data for stress/hormone treatments

steps.description: The heat maps and hierarchical clustering of the gene expression patterns were performed using the HemI software (http://hemi. biocuckoo.org/down.php) (Deng et al., 2014).
steps.searchable_summary: generate heat maps and perform hierarchical clustering of gene expression data using HemI software

steps.description: we performed a hierarchy cluster containing 11 different transcripts, selected from data sets obtained from 57 transcripts, out of 59 CabZIP genes (except CabZIP25) at different developmental stages in the specific tissues (Supplementary Table S5).
steps.searchable_summary: perform hierarchical clustering on a subset of 57 CabZIP genes from the developmental dataset
inputs: Subset of developmental gene expression data (57 genes)

methods.method_name: Experimental Sample Collection for Gene Expression Analysis
methods.description: Preparation of pepper seedlings and collection of tissue samples under specific developmental stages, stress, and hormone treatments for subsequent gene expression analysis (e.g., RNA extraction and qPCR).

steps.description: seedlings were prepared in a natural glasshouse with the daily temperature of 25 to 29°C and night temperature 16 to 20°C.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pepper seedlings under controlled glasshouse conditions

steps.description: The samples of roots, stems, leaves, flower buds, fruits, and seeds were collected for expression analysis of different tissues.
steps.searchable_summary: collect tissue samples from different plant organs for expression analysis
outputs: Tissue samples (roots, stems, leaves, flower buds, fruits, seeds)

steps.description: For heat stress treatment, the seedlings were transferred into a growth chamber at 45°C...
steps.searchable_summary: apply heat stress treatment to pepper seedlings

steps.description: ...and leaf tissues were collected at 0, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 12, and 24 h post treatment, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: collect leaf samples at specified time points after heat stress treatment

steps.description: Salt stress was applied by adding NaCl to a final concentration of 0, 50, 100, 200, 400, and 600 mM in the nutrient solution, respectively...
steps.searchable_summary: apply salt stress treatment to pepper seedlings using NaCl in nutrient solution

steps.description: ...and leaves sample were collected at 6 h post-treatments.
steps.searchable_summary: collect leaf samples at 6 hours after salt stress treatment

steps.description: Similarly, pepper seedlings were sprayed evenly with 0, 1, 10, 50, 100, and 500µM ABA or JA solution for phytohormone treatments...
steps.searchable_summary: apply phytohormone treatments (ABA or JA) to pepper seedlings by spraying

steps.description: ...and leaves were sampled at 6 h after treatments.
steps.searchable_summary: collect leaf samples at 6 hours after phytohormone treatments

steps.description: All the samples were collected in quadruplicates...
steps.searchable_summary: collect all samples in quadruplicates

steps.description: ...frozen in liquid nitrogen...
steps.searchable_summary: freeze collected samples in liquid nitrogen

steps.description: ...and stored at −80°C prior to the extraction of RNA.
steps.searchable_summary: store frozen samples at -80°C

methods.method_name: Generation of Transgenic Arabidopsis and Salt Stress Phenotyping
methods.description: Generation of transgenic Arabidopsis plants overexpressing CabZIP25 and evaluation of their salt resistance phenotype through germination, growth, and root development assays under varying salt concentrations.

steps.description: The full-length CDS of CabZIP25 was amplified from the line R9 using the CabZIP25 CDS primers (Supplementary Table S6)...
steps.searchable_summary: amplify full-length CDS of CabZIP25 using PCR

steps.description: ...and the amplification product was cloned into the Xba I and Kpn I site of pVBG2307 vector to create pVBG2307-CabZIP25 under the control of the CaMV35S promoter.
steps.searchable_summary: clone amplified CDS into plant transformation vector pVBG2307

steps.description: The obtained overexpression vector was transformed into Agrobacterium tumefaciens strain GV3101...
steps.searchable_summary: transform Agrobacterium tumefaciens with the overexpression vector

steps.description: ...and was subsequently infiltrated into WT Arabidopsis plants (Clough and Bent, 1998).
steps.searchable_summary: infiltrate wild-type Arabidopsis plants with transformed Agrobacterium

steps.description: The seeds were screened on Murashige and Skoog (MS) selection medium with kanamycin (50 mg/L)...
steps.searchable_summary: screen seeds from infiltrated plants on selective MS medium containing kanamycin

steps.description: ...and T3 generation lines OE1, OE2, and OE5 were used for experimental analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: select homozygous T3 generation transgenic lines for experiments
outputs: Homozygous T3 transgenic lines (OE1, OE2, OE5)

steps.description: For salt tolerance evaluation of over-expressing CabZIP25, seeds of transgenic and WT Arabidopsis were germinated on MS medium supplemented with 0, 100, and 150 mM NaCl.
steps.searchable_summary: germinate transgenic and WT Arabidopsis seeds on MS medium with varying NaCl concentrations

steps.description: The germination rate was observed every day...
steps.searchable_summary: observe and record germination rate daily

steps.description: ...and the fresh weight... were measured at 10-day-old Arabidopsis seedlings.
steps.searchable_summary: measure fresh weight of 10-day-old seedlings

steps.description: ...and chlorophyll content were measured at 10-day-old Arabidopsis seedlings. The chlorophyll content was measured with the method described previously (Cai et al., 2017).
steps.searchable_summary: measure chlorophyll content of 10-day-old seedlings

steps.description: To assess the effect of CabZIP25 overexpression on Arabidopsis roots development, seeds of CabZIP25-OE and WT lines were germinated on MS petri dishes containing 0, 100, and 150 mM NaCl. The dishes were placed vertically...
steps.searchable_summary: germinate transgenic and WT seeds on vertical MS plates with NaCl to assess root development

steps.description: ...and the root lengths of transgenic and WT plants were measured after 10 days.
steps.searchable_summary: measure root lengths of 10-day-old seedlings on vertical plates

steps.description: Three replicates were performed in this experiment.
steps.searchable_summary: perform the experiment with three biological replicates

steps.description: Data were reported as means ± SD.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate and report means and standard deviations for experimental data
inputs: Measured data (germination rate, fresh weight, chlorophyll content, root length)

methods.description: Investigation of the evolutionary relationship of the CabZIP family within pepper and among pepper, Arabidopsis, and tomato using phylogenetic tree construction and analysis, including classification based on phylogeny and DNA-binding specificity.

steps.description: Obtain bZIP protein sequences for the CabZIP family in pepper (60 sequences).
steps.searchable_summary: obtain protein sequences for the CabZIP gene family
inputs: Source of CabZIP sequences (e.g., genome database)

steps.description: Obtain 20 bZIP protein sequences from the Arabidopsis AtbZIP family to use as subgroup markers.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain protein sequences for Arabidopsis AtbZIP family

steps.description: Perform phylogenetic analysis using the pepper and Arabidopsis sequences.
steps.searchable_summary: construct phylogenetic tree using CabZIP and AtbZIP protein sequences

steps.description: Classify CabZIP proteins into groups (A-I, S) based on the phylogenetic tree, using Arabidopsis groups as markers.
steps.searchable_summary: classify CabZIP proteins into groups based on phylogenetic tree topology and known markers

steps.description: The 60 CabZIPs were also categorized into 10 groups (I-X) according to the DNAbinding specificity of basic and hinge regions defined from the first Leu ( Figure 1B).
steps.searchable_summary: categorize CabZIP proteins into groups based on DNA-binding specificity of basic and hinge regions

steps.description: Construct and compare phylogenetic trees using full CabZIP proteins, bZIP domains, and basic/hinge regions, assessing reliability based on bootstrap values (>50%).
steps.searchable_summary: construct and compare phylogenetic trees based on different protein regions (full, domain, basic/hinge)

steps.description: Obtain bZIP protein sequences for tomato (SlbZIPs).
steps.searchable_summary: obtain protein sequences for tomato SlbZIP family

steps.description: Construct an inter-species phylogenetic tree including CabZIPs, AtbZIPs, and SlbZIPs (total 201 bZIPs).
steps.searchable_summary: construct inter-species phylogenetic tree including pepper, Arabidopsis, and tomato bZIPs

steps.description: Analyze the inter-species phylogenetic tree to identify homologous bZIP TFs and assess evolutionary relationships among the three plant species.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze inter-species phylogenetic tree to identify homologs and evolutionary relationships

methods.description: Analysis of the intron/exon organization of CabZIP genes to understand structural evolution within the gene family.

steps.description: To get insights into the structural evolution of the 60 CabZIP genes, their intron/ exon organization was analyzed (Supplementary Table S1 and Supplementary Figure S3).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze intron/exon organization of CabZIP genes
outputs: Intron/exon structure data for each gene

steps.description: Analyze the exon/intron patterns specifically for the basic and hinge regions.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze intron/exon patterns within basic and hinge regions of CabZIP genes
inputs: Definition of basic and hinge regions
outputs: Intron/exon patterns specific to basic/hinge regions

steps.description: Observe and record the distribution, number, and splicing phase of introns/exons.
steps.searchable_summary: record distribution, number, and splicing phase of introns/exons

steps.description: Identify and note the CabZIP genes that contain no introns and their distribution among groups.
steps.searchable_summary: identify intron-less CabZIP genes and their group distribution
outputs: List of intron-less genes and their groups

steps.description: Analyze and note the variation in the number of introns within the ORF for intron-containing genes, particularly noting variation within different groups.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze and record variation in intron numbers per gene and per group
outputs: Summary of intron number variation per gene and group

steps.description: Compare structural patterns within and between different groups.
steps.searchable_summary: compare gene structural patterns within and between different CabZIP groups
